question.text: What reports does FIU-IND receive from reporting entities?

answer.text: Cash Transaction Reports, Suspicious Transaction Reports, Cross Border Wire Transfer Reports, Non-Profit Organisation Transaction Reports, and Immovable Property Reports
Only Suspicious Transaction Reports and Cash Transaction Reports
Annual financial statements and audit reports from all companies
Monthly trading reports from stock exchanges

explanation.text: **The Logic:** FIU-IND acts as the central point for receiving CTRs (Cash Transaction Reports), STRs (Suspicious Transaction Reports), CBWTRs (Cross Border Wire Transfer Reports), NTRs (Non-Profit Organisation Transaction Reports), and IPRs (Reports on Purchase or Sale of Immovable Property) from various reporting entities. **The Trap:** Option B is incomplete — FIU-IND receives five types of reports, not just two. Annual financials go to ROC/MCA, and trading reports go to SEBI/exchanges. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the five report types: CTR, STR, CBWTR, NTR, IPR. Each serves a specific anti-money laundering intelligence purpose.
